summary: Confirm RP2350-PiZero pinout (HDMI, SD, USB host)
description: RESOLVED from the Waveshare schematic + official demo source (docs/RP2350-PiZero-schematic.pdf, .ref/demo). RP2350B, 520KB SRAM, 16MB flash, PSRAM pad UNPOPULATED. HDMI TMDS on GPIO32-39 (D2=32/33, D1=34/35, D0=36/37, CLK=38/39). microSD SPI: SCK=30, MOSI=31, MISO=40, CS=43, card-detect=22, ~12.5MHz. PIO-USB host: D+=28, D-=29. I2C0 SDA=6/SCL=7, UART0 TX=0/RX=1, WS2812 LED=2. Full pinout in README.md.

summary: Decision: libdvi (PIO) DVI, 320x240 fb hardware-doubled to 640x480
description: RESOLVED. HDMI is wired to GPIO32-39, but HSTX is hardwired to GPIO12-19 only, so HSTX CANNOT drive this board's HDMI -- libdvi (Wren6991/PicoDVI, PIO) is mandatory, matching Waveshare's reference demo. Framebuffer = 320x240 RGB565 (~154KB, fits 520KB SRAM); libdvi scans out pixel/line-doubled (DVI_VERTICAL_REPEAT=2) to 640x480p 60Hz. CoCo 256x192 centers in 320x240 -> 512x384 with blank borders on screen. Config = pico_sock_cfg, invert_diffpairs=false, pio_set_gpio_base(pio,16). See README.md.

summary: DEFERRED: reconcile DVI (~252MHz) vs PIO-USB (120/240MHz) system clock
description: DEFERRED to the USB phase (PIZERO-11+). Decision: prove XRoar rendering first by running DVI at its native ~252MHz TMDS clock (close to the AMOLED port's 250MHz, so emulation gets full headroom and no clock compromise). The conflict only matters once USB host is added: libdvi 640x480p60 needs sys ~252MHz, Pico-PIO-USB needs exactly 120/240MHz (252/48 is fractional -> USB jitter). A custom DVI driver would NOT help -- the bit-rate=sysclk coupling is inherent to PIO DVI. When revisited: feed libdvi a 240MHz-friendly 640x480 timing (24MHz pixel clock ~57Hz, monitor-tolerated) so USB is happy, and verify on a real monitor. [IN-PROGRESS 2026-05-28: spike on path 1 -- sysclk 252->240 MHz with a custom 640x480 timing at 24 MHz pixel clock (~57.14 Hz refresh). Unblocks PIZERO-11.] [DONE 2026-05-28: path 1 verified on hardware. sysclk 240 MHz, custom 640x480 timing at 24 MHz pixel clock (~57.14 Hz refresh) -- monitor accepts, BASIC boots normally, emulator still hits 60 fps (decoupled from display refresh via FRAME_PERIOD_US). Unblocks PIZERO-11.]

summary: Fork reusable skeleton from the AMOLED port
description: Copy board-agnostic code from ~/github/coco-rp2350-waveshare-touch-amoled-18: lib/xroar_core, lib/coco_machine, src/coco_boot.cpp/h, AUTORUN.md, deploy.sh. OMIT board-specific: lib/sh8601, lib/qspi_pio, lib/hal (touch/IMU), src/hw_config.c (AMOLED SD pins), and the AMOLED-rotation parts of the blitter. main.cpp written fresh for libdvi. DONE: xroar_core/coco_machine/psram_stub/coco_boot reused unchanged; AMOLED display/QSPI/HAL omitted.

summary: PlatformIO config for RP2350B + earlephilhower + libdvi
description: platformio.ini: RP2350B board id (-DPICO_RP2350B=1), framework=arduino (earlephilhower). Vendor libdvi (Wren6991/PicoDVI, from .ref/demo Arduino/01-DVI) into lib/; lib_deps Pico-PIO-USB + Adafruit TinyUSB, carlk3/no-OS-FatFS-SD. USB Stack = Adafruit TinyUSB. Keep -O2, build_unflags=-Os, lib_ldf_mode=deep+. Arduino.h before XRoar headers (_Bool gotcha). System clock per PIZERO-02b. DONE (build-verified): board=waveshare_rp2350_pizero, -DPICO_PIO_USE_GPIO_BASE=1 for TMDS on GPIO32-39, DVI_VERTICAL_REPEAT=2, DVI_SYMBOLS_PER_WORD=2. Fixed tmds_encode.S assembly by guarding the SDK config includes in dvi_config_defs.h under !__ASSEMBLER__. Compiles+links to firmware.uf2, RAM 31.7%.

summary: libdvi bring-up: 320x240 test pattern on HDMI monitor
description: Port the hello_dvi reference (libdvi, pico_sock_cfg, TMDS on GPIO32-39, gpio_base=16, vreg ~1.2V) to show a static 320x240 RGB565 test pattern scaled to 640x480p60 on a real HDMI monitor. DVI IRQ/scanout on core1. Confirm stable sync and correct RGB565 colors (watch byte order). Baseline before integrating emulation. DONE: verified on hardware 2026-05-27 — colour bars (black/blue/green/cyan/red/magenta/yellow/white) + white border render correctly at 640x480, confirming pin map, TMDS sync, RGB565 byte order (no swap), and 2x doubling.

summary: SRAM RAM-allocation path in coco_machine
description: coco_machine currently leans on PSRAM for the 64KB CoCo RAM. PiZero PSRAM pad is unpopulated; allocate CoCo RAM from main SRAM (static/SRAM malloc) like the AMOLED port did. Verify against the SRAM budget alongside the 154KB framebuffer + libdvi TMDS buffers. DONE: psram_stub 64KB SRAM pool backs the CoCo RAM; verified by successful link + boot (no PSRAM).

summary: New coco_boot_blit_vdg() -> 320x240 centered, no rotation
description: Re-derive the blitter for the 320x240 RGB565 framebuffer: map the nibble-packed VDG 256x192 buffer + palette into a centered 256x192 region (32px L/R, 24px T/B border), no rotation (landscape native). Feed scanlines to the libdvi colour-buffer queue. Re-derive any lookup tables. Simpler than the AMOLED portrait-rotation math. DONE: coco_boot_blit_vdg_pizero verified on hardware — CoCo image centered with black border, correct colors.

summary: SD mount + ROM load on PiZero SD pins
description: Wire no-OS-FatFS-SD to the PiZero microSD SPI pins (SCK=30, MOSI=31, MISO=40, CS=43, CD=22, ~12.5MHz). Mount the card and load /coco/bas12.rom + /coco/extbas11.rom into the 16KB ROM image via the existing coco_boot ROM loader. DONE: SD mounted and ROMs loaded on the PiZero spi1 pins; Color BASIC reached OK.

summary: Milestone: Color BASIC boots to OK on HDMI
description: Integrate emulator loop + blitter + libdvi scanout: XRoar runs, VDG output renders, Color BASIC reaches the OK prompt on the HDMI monitor. Parity with the AMOLED port's Phase 2. DONE 2026-05-27: Color BASIC boots to OK on HDMI at 54fps (cpu 9.3ms, render+blit 9.4ms per frame). Above the 30fps target; ahead of the AMOLED port (~15fps).

summary: Autonomous AUTORUN.TXT demo on HDMI
description: Get the AUTORUN.TXT autotype + disk/cart demo path working on HDMI (parity with AMOLED Phase 3): @DISK/@CART/@DIRECT directives, LOADM .bin direct-load, autotype queue. Confirm a self-running BASIC/demo on the monitor. DONE 2026-05-27: verified on hardware — dsk finder fixed (skips macOS ._* files, attaches real coco.dsk); sample-sd/coco/autorun.txt autotypes a BASIC graphics demo that self-runs on HDMI.

summary: PIO-USB host bring-up: enumerate a USB keyboard
description:
DONE 2026-05-29 on hardware. Pico-PIO-USB host on the PIO-USB port (D+=28 / D-=29) brought up via Adafruit_TinyUSB, host stack on PIO 1 (libdvi owns PIO 0). Three fixes were needed for a clean enumeration + report flow:

(1) Initialize USB host BEFORE multicore_launch_core1 — alarm_pool_create() spins forever if core 1 is already inside libdvi's DMA-IRQ loop. Init while core 1 is idle, then launch libdvi.

(2) Add -DCFG_TUH_HID_EPIN_BUFSIZE=64 / EPOUT_BUFSIZE=64 to build flags so TinyUSB has a place to land incoming HID reports.

(3) Call tuh_hid_set_default_protocol(HID_PROTOCOL_BOOT) BEFORE USBHost.begin() — common wireless keyboard/mouse dongles need to be talked to in boot protocol for reports to flow without report-descriptor parsing. Doing it via set_default (pre-mount) keeps both interfaces mounting cleanly; trying it via set_protocol AFTER mount breaks the second interface.

Verified: USB-A wireless dongle enumerates (VID:PID 05ac:0256), two interfaces mount (idx=0 boot mouse, idx=1 generic), and [usb] HID report lines stream 8-byte boot-keyboard reports as keys are pressed (e.g. 0x2C = space). PIZERO-12 (HID -> DSCAN) picks up from here.

KNOWN CAVEAT: not all USB devices enumerate — modern USB 2.0 high-speed keyboards/mice (e.g. Keychron K2, standalone gaming mice) do not work with PIO-USB's 1.1-only stack. Period-correct simple wired USB keyboards or wireless USB receivers are the compatible class.

summary: Map USB HID keycodes -> CoCo keyboard matrix
description:
DONE 2026-05-29 on hardware. USB HID boot-keyboard reports are translated into CoCo DSCAN codes and injected via the existing coco_machine_press_key()/release_key() matrix hooks. Verified: typing PRINT "HI" + Enter on a USB keyboard via the wireless dongle yields the expected output in Color BASIC on the HDMI display.

Implementation in src/main.cpp: (1) static 256-entry hid_to_dscan[] lookup mapping HID usage codes to CoCo K_* values with 0xFF sentinel for unmapped; (2) hid_keyboard_apply() diffs the current 6-byte HID keycode set against the previous and issues press/release events for transitions, plus shift modifier handling (HID bits 1/5 of byte 0); (3) per-key release in pump_keyboard() so autotype/serial input no longer clears USB-held keys via release_all_keys().

Notes: works around our test dongle mislabeling its keyboard interface as proto=2 (mouse) by treating any 8-byte report as a boot-keyboard report regardless of declared protocol. tuh_hid_set_default_protocol(HID_PROTOCOL_BOOT) (PIZERO-11) keeps incoming reports in the predictable 8-byte format.

summary: USB gamepad/joystick -> CoCo joystick
description: Map a USB HID gamepad to the CoCo joystick axes/buttons through the XRoar joystick path. Stretch goal after keyboard works.

summary: Dual-core split: emu on core0, video/USB/SD on core1
description: core0 = CoCo emulation; core1 = libdvi DVI scanout (IRQ-driven) + SD/USB host servicing. Pico-PIO-USB host already wants to run on core1, and libdvi scanout runs on core1 too -- confirm they coexist (PIO blocks: libdvi on pio0, PIO-USB on another). Depends on the system-clock outcome from PIZERO-02b. Target >=30fps per the README. DONE 2026-05-27: double-buffered (two 320x240 fbs, core1 samples volatile front ptr once/frame, core0 renders back then publishes) — tear-free, locked 60fps, RAM 89%. Note: fully offloading video to core1 is impossible (libdvi owns core1) and unnecessary (core0 uses 11.3/16.76ms).

summary: Performance pass: instrument timing, tune clock/vreg
description: Add per-frame timing instrumentation (CPU/blit/scanout) like the AMOLED port. Tune the system clock and vreg within the PIZERO-02b constraints to hit >=30fps. Compare real-time % against the AMOLED port baseline (~36%). DONE 2026-05-27: hit locked 60fps. Two core-0 wins: (1) blit skips re-clearing the static border each frame (54->58fps), (2) alpha renderer uses a precomputed glyph->packed-word LUT instead of per-pixel put2 RMW, cutting render 6.4ms->0.56ms. Per-frame core0 = cpu 9.4ms + render 0.56ms + blit 1.5ms = 11.5ms (<16.76ms budget).

summary: Experiment: alternate video device with sprites + extended palette (TMS9918A-style)
description: HEAVIER TRACK behind PIZERO-26. The lightweight, faithful first increment -- a programmable palette that augments the native VDG (GIME-style palette registers) -- was split out into PIZERO-26 and is the preferred near-term direction (keeps the CoCo architecture, recolors existing modes, no foreign chip). THIS issue remains the full 'alternate video device' exploration beyond what a palette indirection gives. DEFERRED / future exploration. Add an optional alternate video mode that emulates a sprite-capable, higher-color video chip in place of (or alongside) the MC6847 VDG, to explore 'what if the CoCo had a different graphics board'. Target: TMS9918A-class features first (32 hardware sprites, 15-color palette, tile/pattern modes) -- period-correct alternative to the 6847; stretch to V9938/V9958 (256 colors, more sprites, per-line palette). Prior art on RP2350 to mine: PICO9918 PRO (visrealm/pico9918) emulates the TMS9918A on an RP2350 with HDMI out (open source); console PPUs already run on RP2350+DVI (pico-infonesPlus NES PPU, pico-genesisPlus/pico-smsplus Sega VDPs). NOTE: those projects drive HDMI via HSTX (GPIO12-19); our board wires HDMI to GPIO32-39 so HSTX is unavailable -- reuse only the VDP emulation core, keep our libdvi/PIO scanout. Implementation sketch: VRAM array + register file + per-scanline renderer feeding the existing 320x240 RGB565 libdvi framebuffer; branch coco_machine's VDG hookup (lib/coco_machine) to route to an alternate video device. Investigate how XRoar models video devices and whether the alternate chip is bus-addressable like a cart/expansion. References captured in chat 2026-05-27.

summary: Experiment: synth / sound-chip audio device (AMY + AY-3-8910/SID)
description:
DEFERRED / future exploration. STRETCH that DEPENDS on PIZERO-18: it reuses whatever audio output path PIZERO-18 establishes (HDMI data islands or PWM) -- do not start until native CoCo audio output exists. Companion to PIZERO-16 (alternate video): add an optional synth/sound-chip 'sound board' for the CoCo, beyond the native 6-bit DAC + single-bit sound XRoar already emulates. PRIMARY candidate: AMY (shorepine/amy), a fixed-point MCU synth library that runs on RP2350 (Arduino support for ESP32-S3/RP2040/RP2350/Teensy; AMYboard hardware is ESP32-S3 but the engine is portable). AMY gives virtual-analog (Juno-6), FM (DX7 6-op), partial/breakpoint (Alles/Atari AMY), wavetable/PCM sampler, and an 808-style drum machine -- expose a few as an alternate sound device. Discrete-chip alternatives (portable C cores): AY-3-8910/YM2149 (libayemu, emu2149 -- cheap, period-correct), SID 6581/8580 (reSID heavy; lighter reimpls -- aspirational), YM2612/SN76489 (libOPNMIDI, ymfm -- FM, cycle-hungry). RP2350 reference: Datanoise PicoADK v2.

PREREQUISITE DECISIONS (gate the work):
1. Audio output path -- our Waveshare board has no audio DAC and libdvi isn't set up for HDMI audio. Choose PWM audio, I2S to an external DAC, or add HDMI audio islands to libdvi. Decide first (analogous to the PIZERO-02b clock decision for video).
2. CPU/core budget -- core0 ~11.5/16.76ms at 60fps, core1 owned by libdvi scanout (+ future USB host). AMY/AY are affordable, SID/FM are not. Likely generate a fixed sample-block per frame on whichever core has slack.

Implementation sketch: a synth/sound device fed either by a bus-addressable register interface (chip emulation, branch coco_machine like the VDG hookup) or by a higher-level note API (AMY). References captured in chat 2026-05-27.

summary: Investigate why USB host port on PiZero refuses to enumerate (hardware-level)
description: DONE 2026-05-29 — no hardware investigation needed. The board supports USB host correctly. The earlier failures were device-compatibility, not board hardware: PIO-USB is USB 1.1 only, so high-speed USB 2.0 devices (Keychron K2, standalone mouse) do not enumerate. A full-speed wireless USB receiver enumerated and streamed HID reports as documented in PIZERO-11.

summary: USB hot-replug does not re-enumerate after disconnect
description:
OPEN / UNDIAGNOSED. Symptom: USB devices enumerate only on a cold boot with the device already attached; unplugging and re-plugging (or hot-plugging a new device) does not re-enumerate.

NO CONFIRMED ROOT CAUSE — nothing has been tested or verified. Two UNVERIFIED hypotheses have been raised, neither reproduced or ruled out:
  (1) Power: insufficient VBUS / inrush current from the development USB-C port. Would predict that powering the board from a battery / powered hub makes hot-replug work. UNTESTED.
  (2) Software: RP2350-E9 input leakage masking the unplug, plus the Pico-PIO-USB state machine holding the J line, so the disconnect event is never seen. UNVERIFIED.

(History note 2026-06-01: an earlier revision of this issue asserted the power hypothesis as a confirmed 'root cause found via user diagnostic'. That was an agent assumption, not a tested result — corrected here to avoid a misleading record.)

NEXT STEP when revisited: actually test. Power from a battery / powered hub and hot-replug; instrument the PIO-USB connection-state path to see whether a disconnect event is ever delivered; bisect power vs software before claiming either.

References for a future deep dive:
  - RP2350-E9: https://hackaday.com/2024/09/20/raspberry-pi-rp2350-e9-erratum-redefined-as-input-mode-leakage-current/
  - tinyusb #3296: https://github.com/hathach/tinyusb/issues/3296 [OBS 2026-06-04 (during PIZERO-29 HDMI work): reproduced the warm-vs-cold distinction -- after a picotool warm reboot (-x) the USB keyboard does NOT enumerate (emulation runs but no input -> game frozen); a COLD power-cycle enumerates it fine. Data point only, NOT a diagnosis: warm-vs-cold points at USB/PIO-USB reset state rather than power, but still UNTESTED and deferred per user.] [2026-06-06: likely surfaces as a CURSOR/SYSTEM FREEZE when typing under load (e.g. a few SOUND commands) with the USB keyboard enumerated (usb=1). Same load driven over the serial console (usb=0) never wedges -> implicates the USB host path. To confirm: when frozen, check whether the [run] serial log goes SILENT (core0 stuck in USBHost.task) vs keeps printing.] [2026-06-06: USER reports REGULAR freezes over the past few days (pre-dates the PIZERO-31 timer fix, confirming it's a standing stability issue, not the timer work). Watching for a repro to capture: when frozen, leave powered and check the [run] serial log -- SILENT = core0 stuck in USBHost.task (USB). Promote priority if it keeps recurring.]

summary: Native CoCo audio output (6-bit DAC + 1-bit sound)
description:
Make the CoCo actually make sound. XRoar already emulates the 6-bit DAC + single-bit cassette/speaker tap internally, but nothing on this board drives those samples to an output -- the PiZero is currently silent. Filed after the 43b reconciliation (2026-05-31): 43b treats native audio as a first-class deliverable (COCO-7 decision -> COCO-19 implementation, see its docs/audio-decision.md), and that split is the transferable lesson. This issue is the basic, in-scope audio path; PIZERO-17 (synth/sound-chip device) is a separate far-future stretch that DEPENDS on the output path decided here.

GATE (decide first, analogous to PIZERO-02b's clock decision; full feasibility scoping in docs/audio-decision.md):
Choose the audio output sink. PRIMARY candidate: HDMI audio data-island packets emitted by libdvi -- sound over the single existing cable, no extra hardware (needs libdvi data-island support, which it currently lacks; investigate cost vs the 240 MHz / 24 MHz-pixel-clock timing already in use). FALLBACK: PWM to a free GPIO + RC filter or a small Class-D amp (simplest, needs a wire/part). Decide before implementing.

IMPLEMENTATION SKETCH: tap XRoar's sound_module / DAC output, generate a fixed PCM sample-block per frame (CoCo native rate) on whichever core has slack (core0 has ~5ms headroom of the 16.76ms budget; core1 is owned by libdvi scanout), feed the chosen sink. Verify on hardware: audible BASIC SOUND / PLAY / key-click at correct pitch with core0 frame time staying under 16.76ms.

[IN-PROGRESS 2026-06-02: prioritized as the next deliverable. De-risk order per docs/audio-decision.md: (1) prove the monitor accepts HDMI audio over this board's wiring before investing in the full data-island port; (2) cost RAM; (3) port data islands + tap XRoar audio. Code investigation underway to ground the implementation plan.]

[FINDING 2026-06-02 (code-verified, corrects an earlier premise): this stripped RP2350 port has NO audio source at all. xroar_core vendors no sound.c / sound_module / sample stream (desktop XRoar's sound pipeline was not vendored). The CoCo audio bits exist only as PIA1-B register state (coco_machine.cpp:122/878) and are currently masked off and discarded. So PIZERO-18 is TWO independent sub-problems, not one: (A) BUILD a correct PCM source -- sample the 6-bit DAC + 1-bit sound off PIA1-B with emulation-cycle-accurate timing (integrate-and-dump resampler keyed to the master clock, or vendor upstream sound.c) into a ring buffer; (B) BUILD the sink -- HDMI data islands (from scratch in our patched libdvi; the riskiest subsystem in the project -- libdvi has zero data-island/TERC4/InfoFrame code, only the IRQ-hazard warning at dvi_timing.cpp:191) OR PWM to a GPIO (simple, needs a wire/amp). (A) is shared and unavoidable; cheapest validation of (A) is PWM, which doubles as the guaranteed fallback. RECOMMENDED SEQUENCING: build + prove (A) first (hardware-independent code), then swap in the HDMI sink with a known-good stream. audio-decision.md's 'XRoar already synthesises the DAC internally' line refers to DESKTOP XRoar and needs correcting.]

[(A) IMPLEMENTED 2026-06-02, build-verified, awaiting hardware listen-test. coco_machine.cpp: new audio section taps the 6-bit DAC (PIA1 PORT A bits 2..7 via PIA_VALUE_A -- NOT port B, which is the VDG-mode port) + single-bit sound (PIA1 PB1), resampled to 32 kHz mono int16 by SLICING coco_machine_run_cycles on sample boundaries (exact-pitch Bresenham, rates x4 so divisor 3579545 is integer). Overwrite ring (2048 samples / 4 KB) drained via new API coco_machine_audio_read()/coco_machine_audio_rate() in coco_machine.h. main.cpp: -DAUDIO_WAV_DUMP build streams a base64 WAV over USB-CDC (markers ---WAV-BEGIN---/---WAV-END---, 9 s window) and autotypes 'FOR I=1TO8:SOUND I*28,4:NEXT' as a deterministic ascending-tone test -- no wiring needed. Both normal and dump builds link. TWO THINGS TO WATCH ON HARDWARE: (1) RAM now 94.3% (was ~89%) -- the HDMI data-island sink (B) needs more (grown DMA lists + packet buffers), so RAM is now a hard constraint; may need to claw back. (2) per-slice cpu->run re-entry (~530/frame) -- verify core-0 frame time stays < 16.76 ms; if not, switch (A) to PIA-postwrite-timestamp reconstruction. NEXT: user runs the dump build, decodes the WAV, confirms clean ascending tones at correct pitch -> then start (B) the HDMI data-island port.]

[(A) VALIDATED ON HARDWARE 2026-06-02. Flashed the dump build, captured the base64 WAV over USB-CDC, decoded + analysed. The dump build also needed -DAUDIO_WAV_DUMP to SKIP the SD autorun boot (it DIRECT-loads spacewarp.bin -> our injected SOUND program went nowhere; first capture was a constant DC level, not a tap bug). With a clean Extended Color BASIC boot, 'FOR I=1TO8:SOUND I*28,4:NEXT' produced 8 monotonically ascending tones (200->230->270->310->370->470->650->1120/1280 Hz), strong +/-20880 square wave -> DAC tap (PIA1 PA2..7), 32 kHz resampler, and capture/decode pipeline all correct. PERF: normal build live timing while running spacewarp (graphics mode) = fps 60 locked, cpu 10.8 ms (+1.4 ms vs 9.4 ms pre-audio), render 2.75 + blit 1.5 = ~15.1 ms / 16.76 ms budget. Slicing approach confirmed; postwrite-timestamp fallback NOT needed. (A) done; remaining work is (B) the HDMI data-island sink, where RAM (94.3%) is the gating constraint.] [2026-06-06: sink (B) substantially done via PIZERO-30 — HDMI audio works, pitch-matched, 48kHz; at the off-spec-timing ceiling. PLAY-timer bug split to PIZERO-31; ACR/monitor-compat to PIZERO-32.]

summary: Programmable VDG palette (GIME-style palette registers)
description: Augment the native MC6847 VDG with a programmable color palette instead of bolting on a foreign video chip -- the preferred, faithful 'extend the VDG' direction (split out of PIZERO-16, which stays as the heavier full-alternate-video track). MECHANISM: a 16-entry palette register file mirroring the real GIME palette addresses $FFB0-$FFBF (6-bit R-G-B, 2 bits/channel = 16 colors from 64). Intercept writes to that range on the CoCo bus; the VDG renderers (render_alpha/render_graphics/render_rg6 in lib/coco_machine) emit palette[index] -> RGB565 instead of fixed CoCo color constants. DEFAULT palette = the stock CoCo colors, so unmodified games look exactly like a normal CoCo and the feature is completely transparent until a program POKEs the registers. AUTHENTICITY: this mirrors a real CoCo 3 behavior -- in GIME VDG-compatible mode, classic VDG-mode colors route through palette RAM and can be recolored live via $FFB0+ pokes; a recolor program would behave the same here as on real CoCo 3 hardware. DECISIONS: (1) depth -- accept authentic 6-bit at $FFB0-$FFBF AND optionally expose an extended RGB565 palette at our own addresses for beyond-hardware color; (2) modes -- text/semigraphics/CG are index-based and the natural fit; RG6/PMODE4 artifact color is emergent from bit patterns, so palette there means redefining the endpoints (a separate, later mechanism); (3) mirror GIME addresses so CoCo-3-aware code just works. BOUNDARY: this is VDG+palette only, NOT full GIME/CoCo3 -- no hi-res GIME bitmap modes, no extra RAM/MMU, no hardware scroll, no GIME interrupts (those remain PIZERO-16). IMPLEMENTATION WRINKLE: PIZERO-15 precomputes glyph->packed-word LUTs with colors baked in; a palette write must rebuild those small LUTs (cheap -- palette writes are rare, once per screen not per frame). TODO before coding: verify the exact GIME VDG-compatible default slot->color mapping against GIME docs rather than guessing. Product context in docs/product-plan.md (the 'open platform you can extend' story; our homebrew game showcases it).

summary: HDMI data-island encoder core (TERC4 + BCH + packet framing)
description: M1 of PIZERO-18 (B) HDMI audio sink. The bedrock, host-validatable with NO board (like the PIZERO-18 (A) WAV host-test). Build a standalone encoder: (1) TERC4 4-bit->10-bit symbol LUT; (2) BCH ECC for the packet header + 4 subpacket ECC bytes (HDMI 1.4 packet structure); (3) framing for the mandatory packets -- General Control Packet (GCP), AVI InfoFrame, Audio InfoFrame, Audio Clock Regeneration (ACR; at our 24 MHz pixel clock 32 kHz -> N=4096 CTS=24000), and Audio Sample Packet (IEC 60958 subframes). Validate on the host against HDMI-spec test vectors / the shuichitakano PicoDVI-audio fork before any integration. Pure data transformation -> no timing risk here; this de-risks the most mechanical part. Parent: PIZERO-18. Blocks PIZERO-28.\n\n[BUILT + HOST-VALIDATED 2026-06-03. New lib/libdvi/dvi_data_island.{h,c} (pure C, no SDK deps -> builds on host and firmware). BCH generator determined deterministically by brute force to match the reference table: LSB-first, poly 0x83 (HDMI ECC). Host test (/tmp/di_test.c, includes the .c to see static bch_table) ALL PASS: (1) bch_table[0..15] == reference bytes; (2) BCH is a real ECC -- remainder of data||parity == 0 for header and all 4 subpackets; (3) ACR carries CTS=24000 N=4096 (32 kHz @ 24 MHz pixel clock); (4) AVI + Audio InfoFrame CEA checksums == 0; Audio InfoFrame SF/SS = 0x05 (32k/16-bit); (5) all 16 TERC4 symbols match binary spec + guard band 0x133; (6) header TERC4 round-trip -- reconstructed all 32 header bits from lane-0 nibble bit2. Compiles in firmware build (GC'd while unused -> 0 RAM until M2). NOT YET wired into libdvi -- that is M2 (PIZERO-28). NOTE: audio-sample IEC 60958 bit positions are best-effort and get verified on the sink in M4 (PIZERO-30).]

summary: Inject benign data island into libdvi blanking without breaking sync
description:
M2 of PIZERO-18 (B). Extend struct dvi_scanline_dma_list with data-island blocks (preamble -> leading guard band -> TERC4 island -> trailing guard band) on ALL THREE lanes, added as extra CHAIN_TO links in the per-scanline DMA chain. CRITICAL: keep the SINGLE back-porch IRQ per scanline -- do NOT take per-block IRQs -- which dodges the documented guard-band hazard (dvi_timing.cpp:191). Pre-encode the per-line packet into a small TERC4 buffer (same pattern as TMDS scanline encoding). For this milestone inject only a NULL/benign packet during blanking. HARDWARE ACCEPTANCE (first monitor test): video stays rock-solid and in sync, fps still 60. RAM: grown DMA lists ~1 KB + packet buffers ~1-2 KB, fits the ~30 KB free. Depends on PIZERO-27. Parent PIZERO-18.

[DESIGN REFINED 2026-06-03 (two de-risking changes vs the original framing): (1) inject during VERTICAL blanking first, not active-line hblank -- vblank lines carry no visible video, so a malformed island cannot corrupt the picture; if the monitor stays synced the mechanism is proven at near-zero risk. (2) BUFFER-SWAP not DMA-restructure: the vblank 'active' region's DMA block already supports pointing at a non-repeating 640px buffer (same as video lines), so fill a per-lane island-line buffer [control fill -> data-island preamble (8px) -> leading guard (2px) -> TERC4 island (32px) -> trailing guard (2px) -> control fill] and point the existing blocks at it. NO grown struct, NO new IRQs, does NOT touch the guard-band-hazard path. Pixel count identical. Budget: our timing h_fp=16/h_sync=96/h_bp=48 = 160px hblank; vblank lines fully blank (640+160 free). Will be flag-guarded (-DHDMI_DATA_ISLAND) so the known-good video path is the default and we can A/B. STILL NEEDED before coding: the fork's exact data-island PREAMBLE control symbols (CTL0-3 for the data-island period differ from the 4 basic vsync/hsync ctrl syms) and guard-band placement -- fetching shuichitakano/pico_lib's line-assembly (dvi.cpp) next.]

[HW RESULT 2026-06-04: implemented + flashed. Pipeline stayed healthy (fps 60, DMA/IRQ fine) BUT the monitor went BLACK with the island injected. Control-only diagnostic (swap buffer filled with pure control symbols, no island) -> VIDEO CORRECT. CONCLUSION: the buffer-swap/repoint mechanism is SOUND; the black screen was the island content. Root cause: you cannot half-do HDMI -- once a sink sees data-island preambles it expects full HDMI framing (video guard bands + preamble on ACTIVE lines + AVI InfoFrame), which our bare-DVI active lines lacked, so it rejected the signal. DECISION: merge M2+M3 -- convert the whole signal to HDMI (video preamble+guard on active lines via the same proven repoint, AND an AVI InfoFrame data island in vblank), then audio (M4). dvi_ctrl_syms confirmed = standard TMDS control tokens duplicated (0xd5354=(0x354<<10)|0x354), so get_ctrl_sym works directly for preambles. Mechanism (repoint blanking blocks at prepared buffers, no struct/IRQ change) carries forward to active lines too. [DONE 2026-06-04: merged into PIZERO-29 (full HDMI mode); the buffer-swap repoint mechanism was validated (control-only diagnostic = correct video) and HDMI-mode video confirmed correct on hardware.]

summary: Switch DVI -> HDMI signaling (General Control Packet + AVI InfoFrame)
description: M3 of PIZERO-18 (B). Emit the General Control Packet + AVI InfoFrame (and the HDMI video preamble/guard bands before active video) so the sink switches from DVI mode to HDMI mode and accepts subsequent audio packets. HARDWARE ACCEPTANCE: monitor still syncs on our off-spec ~57 Hz timing and ideally reports an HDMI (not DVI) input. This is the riskiest monitor-acceptance gate -- if a monitor refuses HDMI signalling on the off-spec timing, fall back to PWM (documented in docs/audio-decision.md). Depends on PIZERO-28. Parent PIZERO-18. [DONE 2026-06-04 on hardware. M2+M3 MERGED: bare data islands need full HDMI mode (a sink rejects island-only = the M2 black screen). Implemented in lib/libdvi: dvi_setup_scanline_for_vblank_island (AVI InfoFrame island in vblank) + dvi_setup_active_hdmi_framing (video preamble + guard band on active lines), both via the validated buffer-swap repoint -- NO DMA struct or IRQ changes. Uses dvi_di_set_avi_infoframe (PIZERO-27). Flashed via picotool load -x (cp-to-BOOTSEL-drive was unreliable). USER CONFIRMED video renders correctly in HDMI mode at fps 60 -- the riskiest monitor-acceptance gate PASSED. Build flag -DHDMI_DATA_ISLAND. Next: M4 (PIZERO-30) audio. Keyboard freeze after warm flash = PIZERO-11b (deferred); cold boot fixes it.]

summary: HDMI audio: ACR + Audio InfoFrame + Audio Sample Packets from the ring
description: M4 of PIZERO-18 (B), completes the audio feature. Feed coco_machine_audio_read() (32 kHz mono, duplicated mono->stereo) into Audio Sample Packets; emit Audio Clock Regeneration (N=4096 CTS=24000 @ 32 kHz with our 24 MHz pixel clock) and the Audio InfoFrame. TERC4-encode on core 1 (where libdvi scanout lives) with a clean producer/consumer handoff from core 0's ring. HARDWARE ACCEPTANCE: audible CoCo BASIC SOUND/PLAY + game audio out the monitor speakers at correct pitch, video still locked 60 fps, core-0 frame time < 16.76 ms. Then re-run the PIZERO-18 (A) ascending-tone test end-to-end through HDMI. Depends on PIZERO-29. Parent PIZERO-18. [2026-06-06 HDMI AUDIO WORKING — at off-spec ceiling; PLAY-melody now plays. (1) Source proven CLEAN via WAV-over-CDC host analysis -> the gap was HDMI delivery, not the resampler. (2) Fixed the 'motorboat': distribute audio across ACTIVE lines (vblank-only burst starved the sink FIFO) — new dvi_setup_active_audio_line + active_line_callback; widened blanking (h_fp 16->14, h_bp 48->130, refresh ~52Hz). (3) DMA-split lanes 1/2 blanking into [fp+sync][bp][active] (DVI_NOSYNC_LANE_CHUNKS 2->3) so per-line audio buffers are bp-only -> freed ~47KB (HDMI build 93%->84%); video re-validated on HW. (4) Rebuilt source resampler as event-driven integrate-and-dump (band-limited, keyed to event ticks via PIA-write hook); removed CPU slicing. (5) PITCH FIX: sink reconstructs the audio clock from the ASSUMED standard 25.175MHz pixel clock, not our 24MHz — ACR CTS=24000 ran ~5% fast (high pitch + FIFO-underrun buzz). CTS=25176 makes pitch MATCH desktop xroar (see PIZERO-32). (6) 48kHz native (N=6144; 231 packets / 77 lines) + dropped the now-redundant output LPF. (7) Dropped bogus PIA1-PB1 'single-bit' tap (DC only; real 1-bit sound is CB2). RESULT: pitch-matched, recognizable music; still slightly more distorted than desktop xroar through the SAME monitor (speaker confound ruled out) — residual is the off-spec 24MHz/~52Hz signal, which is locked (240MHz sysclk required by PIO-USB; 240/25.175 not integer). Ruled out: 32 vs 48kHz, island packing/margin, distribution (all RAM-bounded ~77 lines). NOT yet user-confirmed as 'good enough for V1.0'. All committed.]

summary: PLAY / cursor-blink / SOUND-duration hang — 60 Hz timer IRQ left disabled
description: BUG. FIX APPLIED, awaiting user HW confirm. Symptom: PLAY hangs forever on the first note (tone sustains, command never returns); cursor doesn't blink; PRINT TIMER always 0; SOUND n,d stalls. SOUND-without-duration worked (single tone, no note advance) which masked it; the earlier 'garbage truck' was this stuck first note + the (now-fixed) audio distortion. ROOT CAUSE (serial-driven repro + PIA/CPU instrumentation): the VDG drives field-sync FS -> PIA0 CB1 correctly (fs callback ~120/s), but this port's autorun DIRECT-jumps into a program that leaves PIA0 CB1's interrupt DISABLED (CRB bit0=0) and nothing re-enables it -> BASIC's 60Hz TIMER frozen at 0. Confirmed live: POKE&HFF03,&H35 (set CB1 IRQ enable) -> TIMER advances, PLAY completes and returns. FIX (lib/coco_machine coco_machine_run_cycles): force PIA0 CRB bit0=1 each frame (FS line is physically wired; real BASIC keeps the timer IRQ on). Only bit0 touched — CB2/sound-mux + edge bits intact. Self-verified over serial: PLAY plays the full melody and returns from a cold boot, no POKE. PENDING user HW confirm: cursor blinks, PRINT TIMER climbs, autorun/video unaffected. Pre-existing bug, surfaced during PIZERO-30 audio work. [2026-06-06: fix STRESS-VERIFIED clean over serial — 12 rapid SOUNDs + long PLAY, usb=0: CRB stays 35, no IRQ storm (irq=0, CC.I=0), PC advances through all commands and returns to idle (a7d3 POLCAT loop). The user's 'cursor froze after 3 SOUNDs' was on a cold boot with usb=1 (USB keyboard live) -> attributed to PIZERO-11b USB-host stall under load, NOT this timer fix. Still awaiting user HW confirm that PLAY/cursor/TIMER work AND that the freeze only occurs with USB keyboard use.] [2026-06-06: USER-CONFIRMED on hardware — PLAY plays the full melody and returns, cursor blinks, TIMER climbs. DONE.]

summary: HDMI audio ACR CTS depends on sink's pixel-clock assumption (V1.0 monitor compat)
description: OPEN / V1.0 risk. The ACR CTS=25176 that makes HDMI audio pitch correct ASSUMES the sink reconstructs the audio sample clock from the standard 25.175 MHz 640x480 pixel clock, NOT our actual off-spec 24 MHz. Confirmed on the dev monitor: CTS=24000 (correct for 24 MHz) ran fs ~5% fast (high pitch + FIFO-underrun distortion); CTS=25176 (= 25.175e6*N/(128*fs)) makes pitch MATCH desktop xroar. A sink that genuinely MEASURES the 24 MHz TMDS clock would, with CTS=25176, play ~5% LOW instead — so the value is monitor-dependent. For broad V1.0 monitor support, consider: parse EDID, expose a user-selectable CTS, or accept the trade-off and document it. Parent PIZERO-18/30.

summary: Add core-1 watchdog / freeze detector to root-cause the recurring hangs
description: OPEN. User reports REGULAR system freezes over the past few days (cursor freezes; pre-dates the PIZERO-31 timer fix, so it is a standing stability issue -- likely the PIZERO-11b USB-host path). Add an always-on, low-risk freeze detector so the NEXT freeze auto-dumps the smoking gun instead of needing a live catch. Design: core 1 (the DVI worker, which keeps running via DMA/PIO even if core 0 wedges) watches a core-0 heartbeat counter that loop() bumps each frame; if it stops advancing for ~1 s, dump over serial: core-0 PC, whether it is inside USBHost.task (set a 'in_usb_task' flag around the call), the libdvi SOF/frame counter, and millis(). Optionally arm the RP2350 hardware watchdog as a last-resort auto-reset (gated behind a flag so we can choose log-only vs reset). Keep it cheap and off the hot path. Parent PIZERO-11b.

summary: Even HDMI audio delivery (fill vblank gap) to kill frame-rate warble
description: OPEN. After the ACR-cadence fix the synth sine still warbles at frame rate on the monitor. Cause: bursty region-split delivery -- audio rides only the 480 active lines; the 43 vblank lines carry no audio, so the sink sees a ~1.6ms audio gap every frame. Fix: spread ~116 audio lines (2 pkts each = 924 samp/frame @48k) evenly across ALL 523 lines incl vblank, using bp-only audio islands on vblank lines (the DMA split gave no-sync lines a repointable back-porch block). New piece: dvi_setup_blank_audio_line -- a vblank-line audio buffer whose island trails into CONTROL (no video preamble/guard). Keep ACR on vblank active-blocks (dual island). RAM ~122KB (fits). Behind -DHDMI_EVEN_AUDIO. Risk: intricate per-line framing; uncertain payoff. [2026-06-06 NOT VIABLE on this sink: placing audio data islands in the vblank BACK PORCH breaks video sync -- dual island per vblank line (audio bp + info active) gave green-flash-then-black; single island (audio bp + control active) gave immediate black. Active-line bp-audio works and vblank ACTIVE-BLOCK info islands work, but vblank back-porch islands do not. So the per-frame vblank audio gap cannot be filled this way; even delivery abandoned. Code kept behind -DHDMI_EVEN_AUDIO (off) + the dvi_setup_active_audio_line video_follows generalization. Residual frame-rate warble stands; the ACR-cadence fix (PIZERO-30) was the achievable gain.]

summary: Re-architect HDMI audio delivery using Takano's reference (even per-active-line streaming)
description:
OPEN. Our packet ENCODER is already ported from shuichitakano/pico_lib dvi/data_packet.cpp (lib/libdvi/dvi_data_island.c -- TERC4/BCH/header/subpacket/ACR/InfoFrame), and it's sound. What we never adopted is his DELIVERY/SCHEDULING architecture -- where/when audio islands are placed in the scanline stream -- and that is the source of our residual frame-rate WARBLE.

STATE OF KNOWLEDGE (from this session):
* Pitch fixed via ACR CTS=25176 for the sink's assumed 25.175MHz clock (PIZERO-30/32).
* Source is clean (WAV-verified); the gap is HDMI DELIVERY, not the waveform/resampler.
* ACR cadence matters: 1/frame warbled; ~43/frame (every vblank line) audibly better (PIZERO-30).
* Active-line back-porch audio islands WORK on this sink; vblank BACK-PORCH islands BREAK video sync (PIZERO-34 -- even delivery via vblank is NOT viable here).
* Our limit is RAM: only ~77 pre-encoded per-line buffers of 480 active lines -> bursty delivery (audio bunched in active region, ~1.6ms gap during vblank) -> sink FIFO/PLL wobble at frame rate.

PLAN:
1. Read shuichitakano/pico_lib (and the RP2350 PicoDVI HDMI-audio ports) DELIVERY path: how he interleaves audio sample packets + ACR into the H-blanking of (nearly) every ACTIVE line, and how he encodes per-scanline rather than pre-allocating one buffer per line.
2. Adopt streaming/on-the-fly per-active-line audio encode so audio rides EVERY active line evenly (matching standard HDMI), sidestepping BOTH our buffer-count RAM cap AND the vblank-island sink rejection. Active-line audio already works on this sink, so this is the direction the sink accepts.
3. Re-examine 'Option A' (encode in the DMA IRQ) which blacked the screen early on -- determine whether that was a specific bug vs fundamental, and how he avoids it (which core, timing, handoff).
4. Keep the ACR-cadence fix; spread ACR per his cadence too.

ACCEPTANCE: synth-sine (HDMI_AUDIO_SYNTH) plays as a clean steady tone on the monitor (no frame-rate warble), real CoCo SOUND/PLAY sounds smooth, video rock-solid, RAM fits, core-0 frame budget holds. References: shuichitakano/pico_lib; PicoDVI HDMI-audio forks/RP2350 ports; rh1tech/FRANK (IEC framing, already matched). Supersedes the abandoned PIZERO-34 vblank approach.

[2026-06-07 PLAN + STAGED into PIZERO-36..40. KEY ARCHITECTURE FINDINGS from code review: (1) The per-line bp buffer is sized by the FIXED back porch (bpw = h_back_porch/DVI_SYMBOLS_PER_WORD ~= 65 words, dvi_timing.cpp:458), INDEPENDENT of packet count -- so the pre-encoded double-buffered banks g_la0/1/2[2][77][72] already cost ~130KB at 94% SRAM and the RAM lever is CARRIER-LINE COUNT, not packets/line. The bank model is structurally capped at ~77 lines (120 lines = +74KB, impossible). You CANNOT fix warble by adding carrier lines to the current model. (2) Therefore the only route to even per-line delivery within RAM is Takano's actual model: stop pre-allocating per line; encode ONE 16.16-metered audio packet per active line ON THE FLY in the DMA IRQ into a tiny rotating pool (~4 buffers, ~3.5KB). (3) The original 'Option A' IRQ-encode blackout is almost certainly flash latency: the encoder (dvi_di_encode_header/subpacket + dvi_terc4_syms) is FLASH-resident; moving it to RAM (PIZERO-37) is the surgical fix that makes IRQ encode viable. Spike-measure first (PIZERO-36). All behind -DHDMI_STREAM_AUDIO with the 77-line path as fallback. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/hi-whats-on-the-fluttering-quiche.md]

[2026-06-08 MEASURED (PIZERO-36 done) -- CORRECTS hypothesis (3) above. PIZERO-37 put the encoder in RAM, but a RAM-resident 1-packet encode still costs 4814 ns on HW, which is 89% of the 5420 ns back-porch window -> encoding in the back porch it ships in is STILL not viable even from RAM. So 'Option A' was never going to work as a same-line back-porch encode; flash latency made it worse but wasn't the whole story. THE viable design (4814ns = only 14% of the 33333ns full line): PIPELINE one line ahead -- while line N scans out, encode line N+1's island into a small rotating pool, in the core-1 line IRQ. ~480 enc/frame ~= 2.3ms (~12% of frame), affordable. This is now the hard constraint for PIZERO-38. PIZERO-37 HW-confirmed (audio build runs, fps=52). Stage 0/1 committed (65bb649).]

summary: Stage 0: measure per-line IRQ encode budget (go/no-go for in-IRQ audio encode)
description:
First stage of PIZERO-35. Before any rearchitecture, get HARD DATA on whether a single audio-sample-packet encode fits the core-1 per-scanline DMA-IRQ window. Add a cycle counter (e.g. systick/CYCCNT) around (a) the existing per-line active_audio_cb pointer-store work and (b) ONE RAM-resident dvi_di_encode_header + dvi_di_encode_subpacket call building a single packet. Report each in us against the back-porch window (h_back_porch 130px / 24MHz ~= 5.4us) and the full line (~33us). Decide go/no-go for IRQ-side encode from measurement, NOT the old 'Option A blacked the screen' anecdote (that was flash-resident + 3-packet; PIZERO-37 removes the flash cost). No behavior change in this stage. Parent PIZERO-35.

[2026-06-08 IN-PROGRESS: benchmark implemented + wired to a reproducible env. main.cpp (in the HDMI_DATA_ISLAND block after dvi_di_init) times 20000 iters of the full per-line encode (set_audio_samples + compute_parity + encode_header + encode_subpacket, 1 packet) via volatile fn-pointers (so it can't inline back into flash) and prints '[bench] ... ns/encode' + the budget (back-porch ~5420ns, full-line ~33333ns). Build/flash: `pio run -e pizero_bench -t upload && pio device monitor` (env added to platformio.ini, documented in docs/BUILD.md). AWAITING the user to flash + report the ns number -> go/no-go for in-IRQ encode.]

[2026-06-08 DONE -- MEASURED ON HARDWARE (pizero_bench, RP2350 rev3): one RAM-resident 1-packet encode = 4814 ns (20000 iters / 96283 us). Budgets: back-porch ~5420 ns, full-line ~33333 ns. VERDICT: (a) encoding INSIDE the back porch it ships in is NOT viable -- 4814ns is 89% of the 5420ns bp window, zero margin == exactly why the original Option A blacked the screen. (b) encoding ONE LINE AHEAD on the full-line budget IS viable -- 4814ns = 14% of 33333ns. So PIZERO-38 must PIPELINE: while line N scans out, encode line N+1's island into a rotating pool (Takano's model). Cost at ~480 enc/frame = ~2.3ms/frame (~12% of 19.25ms) on core 1's between-IRQ idle CPU -- affordable. 4814ns is a PESSIMISTIC ceiling (bench calls via volatile fn-ptrs, un-inlined; inlined in the IRQ it'll be less). Side result: confirms PIZERO-37 healthy on HW (audio build boots, [hdmi] M2 active-line audio, fps=52). Bench code/env (pizero_bench, HDMI_ENCODE_BENCH) can be removed now or kept as an encode-cost regression check.]

summary: Stage 1: relocate HDMI data-island encoder + TERC4 table to RAM (no logic change)
description:
Second stage of PIZERO-35. The encoder is currently flash-resident, which is the suspected root cause of the original 'Option A' IRQ-encode screen blackout (XIP latency in the hard per-line window). Mark dvi_di_encode_header (dvi_data_island.c:155), dvi_di_encode_subpacket (:172), dvi_di_set_audio_samples, dvi_di_compute_parity (:39), and the dvi_terc4_syms table (:9) __not_in_flash_func / __scratch_x. bch_table (:19) is already a RAM static. Pure relocation: rebuild, confirm the existing 77-line bank build still works on hardware, and record the RAM delta. Prerequisite for IRQ-side encoding. Parent PIZERO-35.

[2026-06-08 DONE (build-verified; awaiting HW confirm). dvi_di_encode_header/subpacket, set_audio_samples, compute_parity, bch + the dvi_terc4_syms table now in RAM (.time_critical), confirmed in the pizero_audio link map: dvi_di_encode_header @0x200098e8, dvi_terc4_syms @0x20009a78 (flash *_veneer trampoline is expected/fine). RAM delta ~+720B (pizero_audio 92.5%). GOTCHA found + documented (docs/BUILD.md 4a): the arduino-pico core defines PICO_NO_HARDWARE=0, so the SDK's __not_in_flash_func macro SILENTLY no-ops in a C TU -> stayed in flash. Fixed with an explicit __attribute__((section('.time_critical.dvi_di'))) instead. Default silent pizero env still links clean (94.4%). Keep in-progress until pizero_audio is flashed and audio still plays (pure relocation, expected no behavior change).

[2026-06-08 DONE -- HW-confirmed via the pizero_bench flash: the audio build boots and runs cleanly with the relocated encoder ([hdmi] M2 active-line audio, fps=52, DVI up). Pure relocation, no behavior change. Audible-quality listen test folds into PIZERO-38 acceptance.]

summary: Stage 2: rotating bp-buffer pool + IRQ encode + 16.16 sample meter (synth source) behind -DHDMI_STREAM_AUDIO
description:
Core of PIZERO-35: port Takano's even per-active-line delivery. Replace the 77 dedicated per-line buffers g_la0/1/2[2][77][72] (~130KB, RAM-capped at 94%) with a SMALL rotating pool g_pool0/1/2[POOL][72] (start POOL=4, ~3.5KB; frees ~126KB) pre-filled once with static framing (control fill, preamble, guards, video preamble/guard -- factor a helper out of dvi_setup_active_audio_line, dvi_timing.cpp:442). In active_audio_cb (main.cpp:472): advance slot s=(s+1)%POOL, run a 16.16 accumulator (g_samples_per_line16 = (924<<16)/480, reset at v==0) to pick n=0..4 samples, build ONE audio-sample packet, compute_parity, encode header->pool0[s][5..20] + subpacket->pool1/2[s][5..20], then repoint l0[2]/l1[1]/l2[1].read_addr at the slot. RP2350 SRAM is uncached -> no cache barrier, only ordering (pointer store after data writes). Keep ACR/AVI/AudioIF on the vblank active block unchanged (do NOT touch vblank back porch -- PIZERO-34). Gate everything behind -DHDMI_STREAM_AUDIO (platformio.ini:26); flag-off = today's proven 77-line path. Drive with the HDMI_AUDIO_SYNTH 440Hz sine to isolate transport. ACCEPTANCE: clean steady tone, NO ~52Hz warble, A/B vs flag-off; video rock-solid. Depends on PIZERO-36/37. Parent PIZERO-35.

[2026-06-08 HARD CONSTRAINT from PIZERO-36 measurement: one encode = 4814 ns on HW. That does NOT fit the 5420 ns back-porch window (89%), so the IRQ must NOT encode into the same line's back porch it transmits. PIPELINE: in the active-line IRQ for line N, encode line N+1's island into the next rotating-pool slot (full-line ~33333 ns budget; 4814 ns = 14%). Pool needs >=2 slots in flight (line being scanned + line being encoded); size up from there. The 16.16 meter advances per line N+1. ACR stays on the vblank active block (unchanged). Watch core-1 IRQ duty: ~480 enc x 4.8us ~= 2.3ms/frame on core 1's between-IRQ idle CPU.]

summary: Stage 3: feed live CoCo audio via cross-core SPSC ring (IRQ consumer)
description: Fourth stage of PIZERO-35. Switch the IRQ-side encoder (PIZERO-38) from the synth sine to the live coco_machine_audio_read() ring. This moves the ring DRAIN from core 0 to the core-1 DMA IRQ -> core 0 producer, core 1 consumer. Verify coco_machine's ring is a clean single-producer/single-consumer ring callable from IRQ context; if not, add a minimal SPSC read entry point (head/tail indices with __dmb() on the producer side) in lib/coco_machine. Correctness item: no torn reads, no lost/duplicated samples, hold-last-sample on underrun as today. ACCEPTANCE: CoCo BASIC SOUND / PLAY plays smoothly with no frame-rate roughness; video stays locked. Depends on PIZERO-38. Parent PIZERO-35.

summary: Stage 4: tune POOL depth, validate budgets, make streaming the default
description: Final stage of PIZERO-35. Sweep the rotating-pool depth POOL (2 -> 4 -> 8) to find the minimum stable depth (too shallow = IRQ overwrites a slot the DMA is still reading -> tearing). Confirm video rock-solid >=60s (no black/red flashes, no sync loss), core-0 frame time well under ~19.25ms via the [run] fps/cpu/render/blit/aud telemetry (main.cpp:854), and core-1 IRQ headroom from the PIZERO-36 counter. If solid on hardware AND user-confirmed, make -DHDMI_STREAM_AUDIO the default and keep the old 77-line path behind the inverse flag as the escape hatch. Then re-validate real CoCo SOUND/PLAY end-to-end. Depends on PIZERO-39. Closes PIZERO-35 on user hardware confirmation. Parent PIZERO-35.
